mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average in periods of rising industrial production (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average in periods of rising initial unemployment claims (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts signals a weakening supply environment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than the 60-day moving average in periods of declining housing starts (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average in periods of rising federal funds rate (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average in periods of rising real disposable personal income (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average in periods of declining unemployment rate (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production and a 2-month decline in housing starts signals a weakening supply environment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than the 60-day moving average in periods of rising industrial production and declining housing starts (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the federal funds rate signals a easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average in periods of declining federal funds rate (t-test p < 0.05).
